_comment: Shortcut buttons for the HUD action bar. The browser only ever sends an id; the server runs the fixed action here. type=browser focuses an existing Chrome tab whose URL starts with `match` (else opens `url`); type=open hands `url` to the default handler (used for obsidian://); type=exec runs `run` as argv with no shell. {today} in a url expands to the local date. Machine- and account-specific values are written as ${VAR} and filled from .env at boot (see .env.example); an unset var expands to empty. icon picks a glyph from the sprite in public/index.html (git, window, gem, terminal, code, bolt, triangle, vault); an unknown name falls back to the two-letter label. Edit this file and restart the server to change the bar. match is a URL prefix or a list of prefixes tried in order: the button focuses the first already-open tab matching one, and only opens url when none matches. A list matters because services redirect after load, so an exact-URL match can miss the tab it just opened.

shortcuts.label: TM
shortcuts.title: new Terminal window in the project dir

shortcuts.label: ED
shortcuts.title: open the project in VS Code
